title: Smoky Flaky Filipino Chicken Stew
description: A comforting, umami-packed Filipino chicken stew with a hint of smoke, thickened naturally by custard powder for a silky, flaky finish.
instructions: Heat coconut oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add sliced onion and minced garlic; sauté until translucent.
Add diced carrot and bell pepper; cook 3–4 minutes until slightly softened.
Place chicken thighs in the pot, skin side down; sear until golden brown, about 5–6 minutes. Flip and sear the other side for 3–4 minutes.
Pour in low‑sodium chicken broth and low‑sodium soy sauce; bring to a simmer. Add liquid smoke, cover, and cook for 20 minutes until the chicken is tender and easily shredded.
While the chicken cooks, whisk custard powder with 2 tablespoons of water to make a smooth slurry; set aside.
Remove the chicken from the pot, shred or break into flakes with two forks, and return the flakes to the pot.
Stir in the custard powder slurry and cook for 2–3 minutes until the sauce thickens. Adjust seasoning, adding a pinch of salt if needed.
If desired, stir in 1 tablespoon of coconut milk for extra creaminess and let simmer for 1 minute.
Taste and adjust with more liquid smoke or soy sauce if needed. Remove from heat.
Garnish with chopped cilantro or green onions before serving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
